name: Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento
name_original: Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Báguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento
region_intro: Jogging around Anento offers a variety of routes through diverse landscapes. The region features a mix of rural paths, historic sites like castles, and natural spring areas. While some routes present significant climbs, others provide more gentle terrain for a steady run. Best jogging routes around Anento The most popular jogging route is Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento, a 7.0 miles (11.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path starting from Anento Castle, exploring natural springs and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca, a moderate 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This route provides a loop around Daroca, combining town views with surrounding natural landscapes. Local runners also love the Running loop from Cucalón, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 58 minutes. Jogging around Anento is defined by historic castles, natural spring areas, and rural paths. The network offers options for moderate and difficult ability levels, catering to various running preferences. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 runners have used komoot to explore Anento's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Anento?
A: There are 7 dedicated jogging routes around Anento, offering a range of experiences from moderate to difficult. These trails explore rural paths, historic sites, and natural spring areas.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly jogging routes in Anento?
A: While there are no routes specifically rated as 'easy', several moderate trails can be suitable for beginners looking for a gentle challenge. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cucalón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8 km path through rural landscapes, often completed in under an hour.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging trails near Anento?
A: The running routes around Anento are characterized by diverse scenery, including rural paths, historic sites such as castles, and natural spring areas like the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also find routes that offer views of charming towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2094120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Anento area?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena — trail running circular desde el Castillo de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, which starts from Anento Castle and explores natural springs.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running?
A: Several routes pass by interesting landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena&lt;/a&gt; route starts near Anento Castle and visits the natural springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes near Daroca feature the historic town itself and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5765158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features or springs?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its natural spring areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983227069/aguallueve-de-anento-y-arguilay-de-baguena-trail-running-circular-desde-el-castillo-de-anento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento y Arguilay de Baguena&lt;/a&gt; trail specifically highlights the natural springs of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5088573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aguallueve de Anento&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing experience.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Anento?
A: Runners frequently praise the varied terrain and the blend of natural beauty with historical elements. The komoot community has explored Anento's trails over 30 times, appreciating the opportunities to discover rural landscapes, castles, and natural springs.

Q: Are there longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for those seeking a greater challenge, there are several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541589" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Daroca – Puerta Baja de Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 22.5 km trail with significant elevation changes, perfect for experienced runners.

Q: Can I find routes that combine town views with natural landscapes?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of urban and natural environments. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Daroca&lt;/a&gt; provides a loop around the historic town of Daroca, allowing you to enjoy both town views and the surrounding natural scenery.

Q: What is the typical duration for a jogging route in Anento?
A: The duration varies significantly depending on the route's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Cucalón&lt;/a&gt; typically takes about 58 minutes, while longer, more difficult routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541576" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerta Baja de Daroca – Daroca loop from Estación de Daroca&lt;/a&gt; can take over two hours.

Q: Are there any trails that follow old railway lines or greenways?
A: While not directly part of the jogging routes listed, the broader region features the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4592106" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santander–Mediterranean Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, which offers flat, accessible paths often suitable for running. You might find sections of this greenway accessible from nearby areas.
tips:
We are in the beautiful town of Anento visiting its castle, of which the walls remain, a beautiful main façade with its door and a bridge over a moat.
Nearby, we will find picnic tables shaded by pine trees and beautiful wood carvings/sculptures.

Ideal recreational area to disconnect in nature where there is no coverage.
We will find a natural pool for bathing, picnic areas and barbecue, swings for children and a beautiful waterfall.
